title: caladenia longicauda subsp. eminens
intro: Caladenia longicauda subsp. eminens, also known as the Yellow Fingers Orchid, is a rare and endemic species of orchid found in Western Australia. It is prized for its unique flowers with bright yellow sepals and petals, and is often used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which are said to have a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and wellness practices.
